evidence: The paper claims age and Brunnstrom stage are the most influential predictors: "Age and Brunnstrom stage (lower limb) were identified as the most influential predictors" and "age was the most critical predictor throughout the 15-day observation period." However, these magnitude and ranking claims are corroborated by permutation importance (Figure 2: "One minus C-Index loss after permutations"; Figure 3: "Brier score loss after permutations") and partial dependence analysis (Figure 4), both of which confirm the same ranking. The permutation-based corroboration validates that the importance ranking is robust.
